thinking.text:
The user wants me to escalate this to the rewards team. Looking at my available tools, I should transfer this to a human agent who can handle this rewards investigation. Let me use the transfer_to_human_agents function with an appropriate reason and summary.

The reason would be something like "specialized_department_required" since this requires the rewards team to investigate and correct the discrepancies.

tool_calls.Name: transfer_to_human_agents
tool_calls.Arguments:
{"reason": "specialized_department_required", "summary": "Customer Fatima Al-Hassan (User ID: 890389b165) has identified discrepancies in cash back rewards across multiple credit card accounts. After reviewing 47 recent transactions, the following issues were found:

1. Silver Rewards Card - Marriott Hotels ($167.34 on 11/07/2025): Earned 167 points (1%) instead of expected 669 points (4% Travel rate). Missing: 502 points.

2. EcoCard - Patagonia ($128.47 on 11/04/2025): Earned 128 points (1%) instead of expected 642 points (5% Green rate). Missing: 514 points.

3. Crypto-Cash Back - Trader Joe's ($47.83 on 11/01/2025): Earned 47 points (1%) instead of expected 95 points (2% standard rate). Missing: 48 points.

4. Business Platinum Rewards Card - United Airlines ($347.62 on 11/02/2025): Earned 521 points (1.5%) instead of expected 1,390 points (4% Travel rate). Missing: 869 points.

5. Business Platinum Rewards Card - LinkedIn Ads ($512.47 on 10/23/2025): Earned 768 points (1.5%) instead of expected higher rate (similar to Google Ads transaction which earned 10%). Potentially missing: 1,281+ points.

Total potentially missing rewards: approximately 3,214+ points. Customer requests detailed review and correction of all discrepancies."}
